meaning: n.联合的原则,工会主义,联合主义,联邦主义
examples: Many will regard this as a less-than-enticing topic, and some may rate unionism not much racier.
The evidence suggests that when unionism takes pragmatic and progressive positions, it can indeed prosper.
What the Greek organizing committee (ATHOC) was warned about was lethargy, unionism and unrealistic expectations.
The agreement has changed the nature of politics in Northern Ireland and opened a chasm within unionism.
